step_by_step_thinking: \boxed{B} No — Prosopagnosia is specifically defined as the inability to recognize faces, primarily via visual cues. While patients may rely on auditory (e.g., voice) or tactile cues to compensate, this does not mean prosopagnosia itself is synonymous with auditory recognition deficiency. Auditory agnosia is a separate condition involving the failure to recognize sounds, especially voices, and is unrelated to face recognition.
